rationale: Foreign corporation's name in its home (pre-transfer) jurisdiction. Foreign-entity-name convention; same key as MLLC-12 / MLPA-12B / MBCA-2A.

widget_id: The current jurisdiction of its incorporation is
label_verbatim: FIRST: The current jurisdiction of its incorporation is
rationale: State or country where the entity is currently incorporated (pre-transfer). Same key as MLLC-12 / MLPA-12B.

label_verbatim: and the date on which it was authorized to transact business in the State of Maine is
rationale: Original date the foreign corporation qualified in Maine (before the transfer). Same key as MLPA-12B / FICT-4 / CLKRA-3.
Radio option — enum value 'foreign_nonprofit_corporation' (paired with attached MNPCA-12 per FOURTH).
Radio option — enum value 'foreign_limited_partnership' (paired with attached MLPA-12 per FOURTH).
Radio option — enum value 'foreign_limited_liability_company' (paired with attached MLLC-12 per FOURTH).
Radio option — enum value 'foreign_limited_liability_partnership' (paired with attached MLLP-12 per FOURTH).

label_verbatim: THIRD: The new jurisdiction whose laws govern its internal affairs is
rationale: Jurisdiction governing the entity's internal affairs after the transfer. May equal or differ from entity.home_jurisdiction (e.g., re-domestication to a third state during entity-type conversion).

widget_id: type or print name and capacity
label_verbatim: By (signature of an officer or other duly authorized representative) / (type or print name and capacity)
rationale: Combined signer printed name and capacity (Shape D). Form footnote requires §1524.1 authorization (officer or other duly authorized representative).

label_verbatim: Name of entity(s) on the submitted filings [1]

rationale: Cover-letter row 2 (typically holds the bundled Application for Authority's filer entity name).

rationale: Total fees: $70 base for MBCA-12C (per page-0 header) plus the bundled Application for Authority's own filing fee plus expedite premium. Synth/rubric should treat this as a sum, not just $70.
